mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive more often than negative during periods of declining FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening earnings and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is directional because labor-market deterioration reduces revenue expectations and raises recession odds.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is indistinguishable from zero after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS together with rising PAYEMS indicates broad nominal demand and employment growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both sales and payroll expansion because each alone is an incomplete demand signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO while HOUST remains weak implies industrial activity is improving faster than housing supply, supporting cyclical earnings and equity returns over the next quarter. The signal is slow-moving and should work on a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day returns do not follow periods where INDPRO accelerates and HOUST stays below its recent trend, or if the interaction term turns negative in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies policy is behind inflation, which raises the odds of future tightening and compresses equity multiples. S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when inflation rises faster than rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up, rates-low episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the spread between CPIAUCSL trend and FEDFUNDS trend has no predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 signals weakening aggregate output and lower expected corporate earnings, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when consumer sales are also soft.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with falling GDPC1 do not correspond to negative 60-day equity returns more often than chance, or if adding RSAFS does not improve out-of-sample sign prediction.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates tight labor demand and income growth, which supports risk assets and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is fast because labor data surprises quickly reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls or stays below 6.5% while PAYEMS rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-unemployment and payroll-growth condition fails to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is rising while RSAFS is falling, housing activity is diverging from general retail demand, indicating sector rotation rather than broad nominal strength. That mix should produce weak or negative S&P 500 returns over the next month because it reflects narrow growth without broad consumption support.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises while RSAFS declines over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if divergence between HOUST and RSAFS does not precede negative 20-day returns, or if triggered windows show positive returns more than 60% of the time.
